title: Startup leaks 2M customer records via misconfigured storage bucket
what_happened: A fast-growing startup left a cloud storage bucket publicly readable, exposing 2 million customer records including emails and hashed passwords.
timeline: 2025-01: Bucket created with default-open access during a rushed launch.
2025-04: Security researcher discovers the open bucket.
2025-05: Breach disclosed publicly and customers notified.
why_it_failed: Speed was prioritized over a basic security review before launch.
root_cause: No infrastructure access-control review in the deployment process.
lessons_learned: Gate every deploy on an access-control review
Default to least-privilege on all storage
how_alfy2_avoids_it: Watch for: No security checklist gating deploys
Watch for: Default-open configuration left unchanged
Apply lesson: Gate every deploy on an access-control review
Apply lesson: Default to least-privilege on all storage
